rules: explain one idea at a time

lessons.title: Writing your first program
lessons.learning_goal: Student can write a program that prints text with print().

lesson_chunks.goal: Introduce print() and how a program runs top to bottom.
lesson_chunks.approved_explanation: A Python program runs from top to bottom, one line at a time. To show something on the screen, you use print(). Whatever you want to display goes inside the parentheses.
lesson_chunks.check_question: Which built-in function displays text on the screen in Python?
lesson_chunks.correct_feedback: Exactly — print() is how you show output.
lesson_chunks.hint: It's a 5-letter word, and you call it with parentheses: ____().
source_refs: PY101 Week 1, Writing your first program

lesson_chunks.goal: Introduce strings and that print shows the text inside the quotes.
lesson_chunks.approved_explanation: The text you want to print goes inside quotes. Text in quotes is called a string. The quotes tell Python where the text starts and ends.
lesson_chunks.check_question: What does print("Hello") display on the screen?
lesson_chunks.correct_feedback: Right — the text inside the quotes.
lesson_chunks.hint: Python prints what's between the quotes — and only that.
source_refs: PY101 Week 1, Writing your first program

lesson_chunks.goal: Clarify that quotation marks are markers and do not appear in output.
lesson_chunks.approved_explanation: Notice the quotation marks never appear in the output — they are just markers that tell Python where the string begins and ends.
lesson_chunks.simple_example: print("Python")   →   Python   (no quotes in the result)
lesson_chunks.check_question: Do the quotation marks themselves appear in the printed output? (yes / no)
lesson_chunks.correct_feedback: Correct — the quotes are markers, not content.
lesson_chunks.hint: Look at the example: print("Python") shows Python, with no quotes around it.
source_refs: PY101 Week 1, Writing your first program

lesson_chunks.goal: Have the student write their first working line of code.
lesson_chunks.approved_explanation: Now put it together and write your first line of code: the function name, parentheses, and your text in quotes inside them.
lesson_chunks.check_question: Write a line of code that prints the word:  Python
lesson_chunks.hint: Use print(), and put Python in quotes inside the parentheses: print("...").
source_refs: PY101 Week 1, Writing your first program

common_mistakes.remediation: Remind that print is a function call: the text goes inside ().

common_mistakes.description: Student writes print(Hello) without quotes around the text.
common_mistakes.remediation: Text must be in quotes so Python treats it as a string.

common_mistakes.description: Student expects the quotation marks to appear in the output.
common_mistakes.remediation: Show that the quotes are markers and are not printed.

common_mistakes.description: Student writes Print or prnt; Python is case-sensitive.
common_mistakes.remediation: Point out the exact spelling: lowercase print.
